summary: Retrieval parameter tuning. Experiments A and B (k1, ltm_top_k) are answered from existing run data because the long-context run already executed the limiting case of both sweeps. Experiments C and D (k_graph, stm_turns) are not applicable: the LongMemEval eval pipeline has no entity graph and no separate verbatim-STM tier.

parameter_mapping.k1: maps to --top-k (cosine candidate count) in STMSemanticRetriever
parameter_mapping.k_graph: NOT APPLICABLE - eval retriever has no entity graph
parameter_mapping.stm_turns: NOT APPLICABLE - eval uses one FlatHaystackStore, no separate STM tier

experiment_A_k1.method: answered from existing runs - the long-context run is the k1->infinity endpoint
experiment_A_k1.decision: recall crosses 85% between k1=4 and k1=40; interpolated k1 ~= 20 reaches ~85% recall
experiment_A_k1.rationale: smallest k1 satisfying the recall>85% goal. larger k1 buys recall but the accuracy data shows it does not convert to answer accuracy.

experiment_B_ltm_top_k.method: answered from existing runs - accuracy measured at top-k 4, 40, and infinity
experiment_B_ltm_top_k.decision: accuracy is flat (~26-32%, within noise) across the entire range. it plateaus IMMEDIATELY at ltm_top_k=4. adding facts never raises accuracy, only tokens.
experiment_B_ltm_top_k.rationale: ltm_top_k=4 is the Pareto-optimal point: identical accuracy to any larger value, minimum token cost (760 vs 7770 vs 75000). the knee of the curve is at the very first point.

experiment_C_k_graph.reason: the LongMemEval eval retriever (STMSemanticRetriever) has no entity-graph expansion. Continuum's schema supports a graph (migration 004) but the eval bootstrap does not build one. Sweeping k_graph would require first implementing graph expansion - a build task, not a tuning sweep.

experiment_D_stm_turns.reason: the eval pipeline uses a single FlatHaystackStore - there is no separate verbatim-STM tier with a turn count. stm_turns has no knob to sweep.

final_config.note: scorer weights reverted to Iteration-1 defaults - Iteration 2 (0.55/0.20/0.15/0.10) did not improve and dropped recall 4pp.

accuracy_ge_58pct.result: FAILED - accuracy is bounded at ~0.32 by the long-context run; no retrieval parameter can reach 0.58

no_category_below_50pct.result: FAILED - multi-session ~11%, temporal-reasoning ~7% in all runs

latency_p95_lt_500ms.result: FAILED - structural; embedding step alone is ~1800ms

verdict: Retrieval parameter tuning cannot reach the 58% target. The long-context run already proved the limiting case: k1=infinity / ltm_top_k=infinity / 100% recall yields 32.2% accuracy. Therefore no value of any retrieval parameter between the current setting and infinity can exceed ~32%. Three independent retrieval levers - top-k sweep, long-context, scorer weights - have all been tested and all are flat. The bottleneck is conclusively NOT retrieval.
recommendation: Per the prompt's own Fallback Plan, proceed to Prompt 40. The evidence-backed unexplored lever is question decomposition: split multi-hop questions into single-fact sub-questions, retrieve+answer each, compose. This is what the LongMemEval paper's strong systems use to lift multi-session from ~20% to ~55%+. Model swaps and retrieval tuning are exhausted - 6 runs, all flat.
